machinery_genes.quote: LEE1 contains the genes encoding the master virulence regulator Ler, the chaperones CesAB, and structural components of the T3SS, namely, EscR, EscS, EscT, EscU, EscK, and EscL.
machinery_genes.note: Furniss & Clements 2018 review of the LEE; names EscR as a structural component of the LEE T3SS (LEE1 operon). LEE apparatus is conserved across EPEC/EHEC/C. rodentium; same Esc nomenclature applies to this C. rodentium instance.

machinery_genes.quote: LEE1 contains the genes encoding the master virulence regulator Ler, the chaperones CesAB, and structural components of the T3SS, namely, EscR, EscS, EscT, EscU, EscK, and EscL.
machinery_genes.note: Named as a structural component of the LEE T3SS (LEE1). SctS export-gate family per apparatus brief.

machinery_genes.quote: LEE1 contains the genes encoding the master virulence regulator Ler, the chaperones CesAB, and structural components of the T3SS, namely, EscR, EscS, EscT, EscU, EscK, and EscL.
machinery_genes.note: Named as a structural component of the LEE T3SS (LEE1). SctT export-gate family per apparatus brief.

machinery_genes.quote: LEE1 contains the genes encoding the master virulence regulator Ler, the chaperones CesAB, and structural components of the T3SS, namely, EscR, EscS, EscT, EscU, EscK, and EscL.
machinery_genes.note: Named as a structural component of the LEE T3SS (LEE1). SctU autoprotease switch family per apparatus brief.

machinery_genes.quote: LEE1 contains the genes encoding the master virulence regulator Ler, the chaperones CesAB, and structural components of the T3SS, namely, EscR, EscS, EscT, EscU, EscK, and EscL.
machinery_genes.note: Named as a structural component of the LEE T3SS (LEE1). SctK (OrgA/MxiK) C-ring base family per apparatus brief; previously Orf4/Orf15.

machinery_genes.quote: It thus seems likely that Orf5 is a homologue of YscL and FliH, plays a similar role (Table 1, Figure 1) and should be re-named EscL.
machinery_genes.note: Pallen et al 2005 bioinformatics analysis of the LEE assigns Orf5 to the YscL/SctL stator family and proposes the name EscL. Also listed as a LEE1 structural component by Furniss & Clements 2018 (PMID 28760850).

machinery_genes.quote: LEE2 encodes the chaperone CesD, three components of the T3SS machinery, namely, EscC, EscJ, and SepD, the effector EspZ, and one ORF of unknown function.
machinery_genes.note: Named as a component of the LEE T3SS machinery (LEE2 operon). SctC secretin family per apparatus brief; EscC homology to YscC confirmed by Pallen 2005 (PMID 15757514).

machinery_genes.quote: LEE2 encodes the chaperone CesD, three components of the T3SS machinery, namely, EscC, EscJ, and SepD, the effector EspZ, and one ORF of unknown function.
machinery_genes.note: Named as a component of the LEE T3SS machinery (LEE2). SctJ inner-ring family per apparatus brief; EscJ homology to YscJ confirmed by Pallen 2005.

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane secretion-machinery component (translocator/effector switch with SepL)
machinery_genes.quote: LEE2 encodes the chaperone CesD, three components of the T3SS machinery, namely, EscC, EscJ, and SepD, the effector EspZ, and one ORF of unknown function.
machinery_genes.note: Explicitly named as a component of the LEE T3SS machinery (LEE2). Also confirmed directly in C. rodentium: Deng et al 2009 generated a CR sepD deletion in the T3SS-secretome study (PMID 20034934). Functions with SepL in the translocator/effector secretion switch; not an effector or chaperone.

machinery_genes.quote: LEE3 encodes three components of the T3SS machinery, namely, EscV, EscN, and SepQ, the effector EspH, and two ORFs of unknown function.
machinery_genes.note: Named as a component of the LEE T3SS machinery (LEE3). SctV (LcrD/FlhA-family export apparatus); EscV homology to YscV confirmed by Pallen 2005 (PMID 15757514).

machinery_genes.quote: Two known type III structural protein-encoding genes, escN (encoding the ATPase for the T3SS) and sepD, were also deleted.
machinery_genes.note: Deng et al 2009 name escN directly in C. rodentium as a structural T3SS gene encoding the secretion ATPase, deleted to make a T3SS-null control. Also named as LEE3 machinery by Furniss & Clements 2018 (PMID 28760850); SctN ATPase family per apparatus brief.

machinery_genes.quote: This suggests that SepQ plays a similar role to YscQ and FliN - allowing new hypotheses to be framed (Table 1) - and should be re-named EscQ.
machinery_genes.note: Pallen et al 2005 assign SepQ (Orf17) to the YscQ/SctQ C-ring/sorting-platform family and propose renaming it EscQ. Also listed as a LEE3 component of the T3SS machinery by Furniss & Clements 2018 (PMID 28760850).

machinery_genes.quote: mutations in C. rodentium orf4, espD, and escD result in attenuated virulence
machinery_genes.note: Mundy et al 2004 name escD directly in C. rodentium; it is the SctD inner-MS-ring component. EscD homology to YscD confirmed by Pallen 2005 (PMID 15757514). Not in a LEE1-3 quote above because it lies in LEE2 region but Furniss review listed only a subset; organism-direct naming used here.

machinery_genes.quote: This suggests that rORF8 and YscI play similar roles to PrgJ/MxiI as components of the inner rod and that rORF8 should be re-named EscI.
machinery_genes.note: Pallen et al 2005 identify rOrf8 as the SctI inner-rod homologue and propose the name EscI. The C. rodentium secretome paper (Deng 2009, PMID 20034934) independently proposes rOrf8 as the inner-rod component.

machinery_genes.quote: The LEE4 operon encodes the structural needle protein EscF
machinery_genes.note: Mundy et al 2004 name EscF directly in C. rodentium as the structural needle protein (LEE4). SctF needle family per apparatus brief; Pallen 2005 notes EscF is a major needle component.

machinery_genes.role: extracellular filament/sheath extending the needle to the host cell
machinery_genes.quote: LEE4 contains the genes encoding the translocator proteins, which form the tip of the T3SS (EspB and EspD), the needle filament EspA, the needle protein EscF, the chaperone CesD2, the gatekeeper protein SepL, and the effector EspF.
machinery_genes.note: Named as the LEE4 needle-filament translocator. Translocon components are apparatus (host-membrane delivery), not effectors, per apparatus brief. Confirmed in C. rodentium by Mundy 2004 (PMID 15039354) and Deng 2009 (PMID 20034934).

machinery_genes.quote: LEE4 contains the genes encoding the translocator proteins, which form the tip of the T3SS (EspB and EspD), the needle filament EspA, the needle protein EscF, the chaperone CesD2, the gatekeeper protein SepL, and the effector EspF.
machinery_genes.note: Named as a LEE4 translocon pore protein. Apparatus, not an effector. Confirmed in C. rodentium by Mundy 2004 (espD mutant attenuated, PMID 15039354) and Deng 2009 (PMID 20034934).

machinery_genes.quote: LEE4 contains the genes encoding the translocator proteins, which form the tip of the T3SS (EspB and EspD), the needle filament EspA, the needle protein EscF, the chaperone CesD2, the gatekeeper protein SepL, and the effector EspF.
machinery_genes.note: Named as a LEE4 translocon (tip) protein. Translocon components are apparatus per apparatus brief, despite being secreted. Confirmed in C. rodentium by Deng 2009 (PMID 20034934, one of the three high-abundance translocators).

machinery_genes.quote: LEE4 contains the genes encoding the translocator proteins, which form the tip of the T3SS (EspB and EspD), the needle filament EspA, the needle protein EscF, the chaperone CesD2, the gatekeeper protein SepL, and the effector EspF.
machinery_genes.note: Named as the LEE4 gatekeeper. Pallen 2005 (PMID 15757514) assigns SepL to the YopN-TyeA/SctW gatekeeper family. Gatekeeper is apparatus, not an effector or regulator.

notes: System: the LEE-encoded type III secretion system (injectisome) of Citrobacter rodentium ICC168, the murine model A/E pathogen homologous to the EPEC/EHEC LEE. The LEE T3SS apparatus uses the Esc (Yersinia-homologous) / Sep (E. coli-specific) nomenclature and is essentially identical across C. rodentium, EPEC E2348/69 and EHEC O157:H7; per the separated-paper rule, apparatus genes named in EPEC/EHEC LEE papers and in pan-A/E reviews are transferred to this C. rodentium instance, and four of the listed genes (EscN, EscD, EscF, SepD, plus translocators EspA/EspB/EspD) are additionally named directly in C. rodentium (Mundy 2004 PMID 15039354; Deng 2009 PMID 20034934). Core apparatus covered gene-by-gene: secretin EscC (SctC); inner ring EscJ (SctJ); MS-ring EscD (SctD); export gates EscR/EscS/EscT (SctR/S/T); autoprotease switch EscU (SctU); major export ring EscV (SctV); ATPase EscN (SctN); stator EscL (SctL); C-ring EscK (SctK) and SepQ/EscQ (SctQ); inner rod EscI/rOrf8 (SctI); needle EscF (SctF); needle filament EspA; translocon EspB/EspD; gatekeeper SepL (SctW). Deliberately EXCLUDED as non-machinery per the apparatus brief: secreted effectors (this instance's curated effectors plus Tir, Map, EspF/G/H/J/Z and Nle* effectors), translocated chaperones (CesT, CesD, CesD2, CesAB, CesF), and transcriptional regulators (Ler, GrlA/GrlR). The ATPase-stalk EscO (SctO) homologue was not given an explicit naming quote in the sources consulted and is therefore not listed (the one component plausibly missing). Status COMPLETE: every canonical SctC/J/D/R/S/T/U/V/N/L/Q/K/I/F core component plus translocon and gatekeeper is documented with a verbatim naming quote. All four PMIDs/DOIs verified via get_article_metadata / convert_article_ids.
